name: Housing Opportunities Of Beaver County, Incor
description_short: HUD-certified nonprofit housing counselor serving Beaver, Lawrence, and Mercer counties in Pennsylvania with free pre-purchase counseling, foreclosure prevention, and closing cost assistance grants.
description_long:
Housing Opportunities Inc. (HOI) is a HUD-certified housing services provider established to improve housing trends across Beaver, Lawrence, and Mercer counties in southwestern Pennsylvania. The organization operates as a nonprofit dedicated to serving low and moderate-income families seeking homeownership and housing stability. With a team of housing counselors, development specialists, and workforce directors, HOI has expanded beyond traditional counseling to become an active affordable housing developer and property rehabilitator.

HOI offers a comprehensive suite of free services including pre- and post-purchase housing counseling, foreclosure prevention negotiations with lenders, financial planning and education, first-time homebuyer assistance, and closing cost assistance grants. The organization provides access to grants and low-interest loans for qualified buyers, operates as a USDA Rural Development program partner to help borrowers qualify for higher-priced homes in designated areas, and manages a workforce training program offering hands-on construction and property maintenance instruction with job placement support. Additionally, HOI actively develops and rehabilitates properties, converting blighted or vacant units into affordable housing for community members.

What distinguishes HOI is its integrated approach combining financial counseling with direct property development and workforce training. The organization recently launched a Location-Based Closing Cost Assistance Grant specifically targeting homes in HUD Qualified Census Tracts to promote homeownership in traditionally rental-dominated neighborhoods. This program received support from the Pennsylvania Housing Finance Agency (PHFA). HOI also operates under Pennsylvania's distressed homeowner assistance program, one of only 18 counties statewide authorized to negotiate with lenders on behalf of homeowners facing foreclosure after an intent-to-foreclose notice is filed.

HOI is best suited for low-to-moderate income families in its service area seeking free housing counseling and education. The primary limitation is geographic restriction to Beaver, Lawrence, and Mercer counties in Pennsylvania. While all counseling and financial planning services are free, access to grants and loan products may have income restrictions and require counseling participation. Potential borrowers should contact directly to verify eligibility and current program availability.
pros: All counseling and financial planning services are completely free—no hidden fees or charges
HUD-certified housing counselors with expertise in pre-purchase guidance, foreclosure prevention, and financial education
Unique Location-Based Closing Cost Assistance Grant for homes in HUD Qualified Census Tracts, backed by PHFA partnership
USDA Rural Development program partner status enables qualification for higher-priced homes in designated Beaver and Lawrence County areas
Active affordable housing developer that rehabilitates blighted properties and converts them to affordable ownership opportunities
Integrated workforce training program offering hands-on construction/maintenance instruction with job placement support
Operates under Pennsylvania's distressed homeowner program—can negotiate with lenders on behalf of borrowers facing foreclosure
Extended hours including evening appointments available beyond standard Monday-Friday 9am-4:45pm schedule
cons: Service area is limited to only Beaver, Lawrence, and Mercer counties in Pennsylvania; cannot help residents outside these areas
Specific income limits and eligibility requirements for grants and loans are not detailed on website—requires direct contact to verify qualification
No information provided about response time for counseling appointments or current wait periods during high-demand periods
Limited transparency about loan terms, interest rates, and specific grant amounts available through their programs
Property development and rehabilitation services appear to be ongoing but limited in scope—not positioned as a primary service offering
best_for: First-time homebuyers with low-to-moderate income in Beaver, Lawrence, or Mercer counties seeking free pre-purchase counseling and guidance
Distressed homeowners facing foreclosure in Pennsylvania counties with negotiation assistance and loss-mitigation counseling
Low-income families seeking closing cost assistance grants, particularly in HUD Qualified Census Tracts
Job seekers interested in construction and property maintenance training with direct placement support
diagnosis: Housing Opportunities Inc. is best for low-to-moderate income families in Beaver, Lawrence, or Mercer counties, Pennsylvania who need free HUD-certified housing counseling, foreclosure prevention assistance, or closing cost help. The main caveat is strict geographic limitation to these three counties; residents outside this area cannot access services, and specific eligibility requirements and program terms require direct contact to verify.
services: HUD-certified housing counseling and financial education
Pre-purchase homebuyer education and guidance for first-time buyers
Foreclosure prevention counseling and lender negotiation
Closing Cost Assistance Grants for homes in HUD Qualified Census Tracts
First-time homebuyer grants and low-interest loan access
USDA Rural Development program partnership lending in qualified areas
Home repair and rehabilitation assistance for existing homeowners
Affordable housing property development and rehabilitation
Construction and property maintenance workforce training and job placement
Mortgage resolution and loan modification consultation
typical_results_timeline: Counseling available within 1-2 weeks of contact.
